org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph

## Interface ExecutionGraph

  * All Superinterfaces:
    [AccessExecutionGraph](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/AccessExecutionGraph.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph"), [JobStatusProvider](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/JobStatusProvider.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph")

All Known Implementing Classes:
    [DefaultExecutionGraph](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/DefaultExecutionGraph.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph")

* * *




        public interface ExecutionGraph
        extends [AccessExecutionGraph](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/AccessExecutionGraph.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph")

The execution graph is the central data structure that coordinates the distributed execution of a data flow. It keeps representations of each parallel task, each intermediate stream, and the communication between them.

The execution graph consists of the following constructs:

    * The [`ExecutionJobVertex`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/ExecutionJobVertex.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph") represents one vertex from the JobGraph (usually one operation like "map" or "join") during execution. It holds the aggregated state of all parallel subtasks. The ExecutionJobVertex is identified inside the graph by the [`JobVertexID`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/jobgraph/JobVertexID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph"), which it takes from the JobGraph's corresponding JobVertex.
    * The [`ExecutionVertex`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/ExecutionVertex.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph") represents one parallel subtask. For each ExecutionJobVertex, there are as many ExecutionVertices as the parallelism. The ExecutionVertex is identified by the ExecutionJobVertex and the index of the parallel subtask
    * The [`Execution`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/Execution.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph") is one attempt to execute a ExecutionVertex. There may be multiple Executions for the ExecutionVertex, in case of a failure, or in the case where some data needs to be recomputed because it is no longer available when requested by later operations. An Execution is always identified by an [`ExecutionAttemptID`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/ExecutionAttemptID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph"). All messages between the JobManager and the TaskManager about deployment of tasks and updates in the task status always use the ExecutionAttemptID to address the message receiver.

    `[getAllVertices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/AccessExecutionGraph.html#getAllVertices-->)` in interface `[AccessExecutionGraph](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/AccessExecutionGraph.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph")`
Returns:
    map containing all job vertices for this execution graph
      * #### getNumberOfRestarts

            long getNumberOfRestarts()

Gets the number of restarts, including full restarts and fine grained restarts. If a recovery is currently pending, this recovery is included in the count.

Returns:
    The number of restarts so far
      * #### getTotalNumberOfVertices

            int getTotalNumberOfVertices()

            [Map](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/Map.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.util")<[IntermediateDataSetID](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/jobgraph/IntermediateDataSetID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph"),[IntermediateResult](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/IntermediateResult.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph")> getAllIntermediateResults()

      * #### aggregateUserAccumulators

            [Map](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/Map.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.util")<[String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang"),[OptionalFailure](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/util/OptionalFailure.html> "class in org.apache.flink.util")<[Accumulator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/accumulators/Accumulator.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.accumulators")<?,?>>> aggregateUserAccumulators()

Merges all accumulator results from the tasks previously executed in the Executions.

Returns:
    The accumulator map
      * #### updateAccumulators

            void updateAccumulators([AccumulatorSnapshot](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/accumulators/AccumulatorSnapshot.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.accumulators") accumulatorSnapshot)

Updates the accumulators during the runtime of a job. Final accumulator results are transferred through the UpdateTaskExecutionState message.

Parameters:
    `accumulatorSnapshot` \- The serialized flink and user-defined accumulators

            void attachJobGraph([List](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/List.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.util")<[JobVertex](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/jobgraph/JobVertex.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph")> topologicallySorted)
                         throws [JobException](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/JobException.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime")

Throws:
    `[JobException](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/JobException.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime")`
      * #### transitionToRunning

            void transitionToRunning()

      * #### cancel

            void cancel()

      * #### suspend

            void suspend([Throwable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Throwable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") suspensionCause)

Suspends the current ExecutionGraph.

The JobStatus will be directly set to [`JobStatus.SUSPENDED`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobStatus.html#SUSPENDED>) iff the current state is not a terminal state. All ExecutionJobVertices will be canceled and the onTerminalState() is executed.

The [`JobStatus.SUSPENDED`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobStatus.html#SUSPENDED>) state is a local terminal state which stops the execution of the job but does not remove the job from the HA job store so that it can be recovered by another JobManager.

Parameters:
    `suspensionCause` \- Cause of the suspension
      * #### failJob

            void failJob([Throwable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Throwable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") cause,
                         long timestamp)

      * #### getTerminationFuture

            [CompletableFuture](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/concurrent/CompletableFuture.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.util.concurrent")<[JobStatus](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobStatus.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.api.common")> getTerminationFuture()

Returns the termination future of this [`ExecutionGraph`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/ExecutionGraph.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph"). The termination future is completed with the terminal [`JobStatus`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobStatus.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.api.common") once the ExecutionGraph reaches this terminal state and all [`Execution`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/Execution.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph") have been terminated.

Returns:
    Termination future of this [`ExecutionGraph`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/ExecutionGraph.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph").

      * #### updateState

            boolean updateState([TaskExecutionStateTransition](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/TaskExecutionStateTransition.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.executiongraph") state)

Updates the state of one of the ExecutionVertex's Execution attempts. If the new status if "FINISHED", this also updates the accumulators.

Parameters:
    `state` \- The state update.
Returns:
    True, if the task update was properly applied, false, if the execution attempt was not found.
      * #### notifyPartitionDataAvailable

            void notifyPartitionDataAvailable([ResultPartitionID](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/io/network/partition/ResultPartitionID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.io.network.partition") partitionId)

Mark the data of a result partition to be available. Note that only PIPELINED partitions are accepted because it is for the case that a TM side PIPELINED result partition has data produced and notifies JM.

Parameters:
    `partitionId` \- specifying the result partition whose data have become available
